description_long: This triumphal arch is a stone structure featuring two robust cylindrical columns on each side, supporting a large central archway. The columns are detailed with horizontal banding and are topped with ornate capitals that display intricate decorative reliefs. The arch itself is broad and rounded, forming a prominent passageway beneath. The stone surface appears weathered, with a matte, rough finish typical of historical monuments. The overall design is symmetrical, with no visible inscription panels but clear classical influences in the column shapes and arch proportions. The structure is open in the center, allowing passage through the archway.
description: A weathered stone triumphal arch with two cylindrical columns on each side, ornate capitals, and a large central rounded archway.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the archway, both columns, and the decorative capitals clearly.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view, still showing both columns and the arch, with more depth visible in the archway.
description_view_2: Another rotated view, similar to the previous, with the archway and columns still prominent.
description_view_3: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, showing the archway and columns from the opposite side.
